_meta.description: Distributed-AI model map for the office simulation (replaces the single-Gemini-engine v0.1.0). Routine per-case agent work (agents 1-4, 5-9, 11) runs on Groq's free tier (agents/workers/groq-client.js callGroq(), agent-base.js queryGemini()) — this is the fix for the recurring Gemini 429s documented in TOKEN-BUDGET.md. Cloudflare Workers AI handles lightweight case routing/classification (callCFRouter()) and is the same-session fallback when Groq is unavailable or Gemini returns 429 (callCloudflareFallback()), via the Worker's native AI binding (wrangler.toml [ai] binding = "AI") — no extra credentials. Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite (per CLAUDE.md 'Launch Decisions' — gemini-3.5-flash is deprecated, do not reintroduce it) is reserved for monthly/quarterly/semi-yearly/yearly report synthesis (meeting-engine.js). The app's AI Search bar always uses Claude (data-center-api). Agent 10 (Architect) never calls an AI for routine cases — see agent-runner.js processCaseBatch()'s Agent-10 special case.

claude_cap_rationale: 30 calls/day = ~$6/month. Distributed across 10 active agents by model_usage_rate. Primary purpose: model training through realistic IT interactions.
description: Groq and CF AI are free — run agents hard. Claude API is paid — cap at 30 calls/day maximum across ALL agents combined, distributed per-agent by model_usage_rate so the model gets meaningful, spread-out training exposure rather than being front-run by whichever agent runs first.

_meta.description: SEPARATE budget/routing from the office-simulation economy above (primary_case_model/claude_daily_cap etc., which governs the 11-agent case simulation and is untouched by this block). This governs the TODO.md-driven chore/content-generation automation (see config/daily-schedule.json's 00:00-06:00 night sweep and the Notebook-X/data-center/archive-alpha rotation) — see workers/model-router.js for the selection logic that reads this config.
_meta.relationship_to_case_sim_cap: Independent budgets. The 30-calls/day case-simulation cap and this $4.50/month chore-automation cap are tracked separately (different D1 tables: interactions.model_source counts vs. claude_budget_usage) and do not share or offset each other.

chore_automation.claude_scope: code-writing tasks and approval/review decisions only, by default. Notebook-X overrides this scope — see notebook_x_override below.
chore_automation.claude_pricing_note: Estimated at current Sonnet 5 intro pricing: $2/M input tokens, $10/M output tokens (workers/model-router.js CLAUDE_PRICING). This changes to $3/M input / $15/M output after 2026-08-31 — model-router.js already branches on that date, but double check the real published price at that time before trusting the estimate.
chore_automation.gemini_role: expanded — default writer for routine content generation across chore-automation tasks (previously reserved for report synthesis only in the office-simulation economy above).
chore_automation.groq_role: routine/easy sub-tasks only (simple formatting, short lookups, classification) — not full content generation.

notebook_x_override.applies_to: notebook-x project only — other projects (data-center, archive-alpha) use the general chore_automation economy above, unchanged.
notebook_x_override.claude_when: task complexity/quality genuinely demands it. Still drawn from the SAME $4.50/mo claude_budget_usd_per_month cap above — this override does not add a second budget.
notebook_x_override.groq_scope: clearly 'easy' sub-tasks only within Notebook-X work (e.g. simple formatting, short lookups) — never content generation itself.
